<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.addNode(observer);
    graph.addNode(target);
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 3. Enable thread safety if requested
    if (enableSafety) {
        threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    
    // 4. Return thread safety status
    return threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition

int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {

    graph.unregisterActiveBatch(batchId);
    
    // 7. Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    bool notInBatch1 = !node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool notInBatch2 = !node2->isInActiveBatch();
    
    // 8. Return success status
    return notInBatch1 && notInBatch2;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;

    }

        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;

namespace test_reaction {

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }

        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

    int m_callCount = 0;
};

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship - source observes target
    graph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // Step 4: Notify target and verify relationship works
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Verify source was notified
    if (!source->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Step 5: Reset observer for next test
    source->reset();
    
    // Step 6: Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    bool success = false;
    try {
        // Transactional update: source observes itself (creating cycle)
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(source, {source});
        // If we reach here, no exception was thrown - cycle was created
        success = false;
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        // Cycle exception caught - transaction was rolled back
        success = true;
    }
    
    // Step 8: Notify target again
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 9: Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!source->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    return success;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    observers.reserve(observerNames.size());
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph->addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int total_calls = 0;
    bool all_notified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        total_calls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
            all_notified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return {total_calls, all_notified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.addNode(target);
    graph.addNode(observer1);
    graph.addNode(observer2);
    
    // Set up chain relationships: target -> observer1 -> observer2
    target->addOneObserver(observer1);
    observer1->addOneObserver(observer2);
    
    // Reset nodes to clear any previous state
    target->reset();
    observer1->reset();
    observer2->reset();
    
    // Notify target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify both observers were notified
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->getCallCount() > 0 && observer1->wasChanged();
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->getCallCount() > 0 && observer2->wasChanged();
    
    // Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    // Chain status: both observers were notified and total calls match expected behavior
    bool chainStatus = observer1Notified && observer2Notified && (totalCalls == 3);
    
    return {totalCalls, chainStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

/**

    // 5. Return captured value
    return captured_value;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive update chain with multiple dependent calculations
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param newA New value for a
 * @return int The result of the final calculation after updating a
 */
int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var1 = reaction::var(a);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(b);
    
    // Create first calculation: sum of var1 and var2
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var1, var2);
    
    // Create second calculation: multiply calc1 by 2
    auto calc2 = reaction::calc([](int x) { return x * 2; }, calc1);
    
    // Update var1 to newA
    var1.value(newA);
    
    // Return final calculation result
    return calc2.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test resetting a calculation node with new dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param c Third input value
 * @return int The result after resetting calculation to use different dependencies
 */
int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Create initial calculation: a + b
    auto calc = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) { return x + y; }, var_a, var_b);
    
    // Reset calculation to use new dependencies: a + b + c
    calc.reset([](int x, int y, int z) { return x + y + z; }, var_a, var_b, var_c);
    
    // Return the result after reset
    return calc.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.value(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.value(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto info = reaction::calc([](const PersonRecord& p) {
        return "Name: " + p.getName() + ", Age: " + std::to_string(p.getAge());
    }, person);
    
    // Update name field
    person.setName(newName);
    
    // Update age field
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return updated info
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recompute_count = 0;
    
    // Create calculation that depends on a and b
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recompute_count++;
        return a() + b();
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int count_after_non_batch = recompute_count;
    
    // Create batch context and execute
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    }).execute();
    
    // Record recompute count after batch updates
    int count_after_batch = recompute_count;
    
    // Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(sum_calc(), count_after_non_batch, count_after_batch);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA, "varA");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB, "varB");
    
    // Create calculations with names
    auto calcAB = reaction::calc([](int a, int b) { return a + b; }, varA, varB, "calcAB");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto actionAB = reaction::action([](int a, int b) { return a * b; }, varA, varB, "actionAB");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::string nameVarA = varA.getName();
    std::string nameVarB = varB.getName();
    std::string nameCalc = calcAB.getName();
    std::string nameAction = actionAB.getName();
    
    // Return combined names string
    return nameVarA + "," + nameVarB + "," + nameCalc + "," + nameAction;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable (sensor)
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Track recomputation count and last processed value
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    double lastProcessedValue = 0.0;
    
    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto status = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        lastProcessedValue = sensor.get();
        
        // Determine status based on level threshold
        if (lastProcessedValue > levelThreshold) {
            return "HIGH";
        } else {
            return "NORMAL";
        }
    });
    
    // 4. Apply filter (only trigger when change exceeds diffThreshold)
    status.filter([&]() {
        double currentValue = sensor.get();
        double previousValue = lastProcessedValue;
        return std::abs(currentValue - previousValue) >= diffThreshold;
    });
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensor.value(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int firstCount = recomputeCount;
    
    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensor.value(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int secondCount = recomputeCount;
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = status.get();
    
    // 10. Return results
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, firstCount, secondCount);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
    int getNotificationCount() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }

private:
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_score;
    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // Step 1: Create ProfileFields object
    ProfileFields profileFields(initialScore, true);
    
    // Step 2: Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance and register owner node
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);
    
    // Step 4: Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 5: Bind fields to owner node
    fieldGraph.bindField(&profileFields, profileFields.m_score.getId(), &ownerNode);
    fieldGraph.bindField(&profileFields, profileFields.m_active.getId(), &ownerNode);
    
    // Step 6: Update score field
    profileFields.score() += scoreDelta;
    
    // Step 7: Record notification count after score update
    int scoreUpdateCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 8: Update active field
    profileFields.active() = false;
    
    // Step 9: Record notification count after status update
    int statusUpdateCount = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 10: Return notification counts
    return FieldBindingResult{scoreUpdateCount, statusUpdateCount};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Helper types
// ============================================

class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct RollbackObservation {
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding;
    int notificationsAfterRollback;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    // Create RecordingNode instances
    auto sensor1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto sensor2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.addNode(sensor1);
    graph.addNode(sensor2);
    
    // Set up initial dependency: sensor2 observes sensor1
    sensor2->updateObservers([sensor1]() { return sensor1->getValue(); });
    
    // Save node state for rollback
    auto rollbackFunc = graph.saveNodeStateForRollback(sensor2);
    
    // Replace dependencies transactionally: sensor2 now observes a constant value
    sensor2->updateObservers([] { return 42; });
    
    // Trigger notification on second sensor
    sensor2->valueChanged();
    
    // Record notification count after second binding
    int notificationsAfterSecondBinding = sensor2->notificationCount();
    
    // Execute rollback
    rollbackFunc();
    
    // Trigger notification on first sensor
    sensor1->valueChanged();
    
    // Record notification count after rollback
    int notificationsAfterRollback = sensor2->notificationCount();
    
    return RollbackObservation{
        notificationsAfterSecondBinding,
        notificationsAfterRollback
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: variable + increment
    auto reactiveExpr = reaction::create(reactiveVar + increment);
    
    // Create reactive calculation: square of the variable
    auto reactiveCalc = reaction::create([reactiveVar]() { 
        return reactiveVar() * reactiveVar(); 
    });
    
    // Get initial expression value
    int initialExprValue = reactiveExpr();
    
    // Update reactive variable
    reactiveVar.value(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated expression value
    int updatedExprValue = reactiveExpr();
    
    // Get squared value after update
    int squaredValue = reactiveCalc();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExprValue,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExprValue,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squaredValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 However, note that the `collectObservers` function might not collect the transitive observer if the graph does not support transitivity?
 But according to the problem statement, we are setting up both direct and transitive relationships, so we expect the transitive observer to be collected.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {
    }
    
    const std::string& name() const noexcept {
        return m_name;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
};

struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Create nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("source");
    auto direct_observer = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("direct_observer");
    auto transitive_observer = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("transitive_observer");
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    observer_graph.registerNode(source);
    observer_graph.registerNode(direct_observer);
    observer_graph.registerNode(transitive_observer);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship
    observer_graph.addObserver(source, direct_observer);
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    observer_graph.addObserver(direct_observer, transitive_observer);
    
    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Collect observers from source
    observer_graph.collectObservers(source, observers);
    
    // Check if direct observer is in collected set
    bool has_direct = observers.contains(direct_observer.get());
    
    // Check if transitive observer is in collected set
    bool has_transitive = observers.contains(transitive_observer.get());
    
    // Return collection summary
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observers.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = has_direct,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = has_transitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>We are given the function signature and the expected steps.
 We need to implement the function `test_thread_manager_multithread` in the namespace `test_reaction`.
 The function should return a `ThreadSafetyStatus` struct with the following fields:
   - safetyBefore: the thread safety status before creating the worker thread
   - safetyAfter: the thread safety status after the worker thread has been joined
   - threadCountBefore: the number of threads registered before creating the worker thread
   - threadCountAfter: the number of threads registered after joining the worker thread

 Steps:
 1. Get the thread manager instance (using `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance()`)
 2. Reset the thread manager state (using `resetForTesting` method)
 3. Register the current thread (using `registerThread` method)
 4. Get the thread count and safety status before creating the worker thread:
      threadCountBefore = `getThreadCount()`
      safetyBefore = `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
 5. Create and start a worker thread that also registers itself (by calling `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread()` inside the thread)
 6. Wait for the worker thread to finish
 7. Get the thread count and safety status after the worker thread has been joined:
      threadCountAfter = `getThreadCount()`
      safetyAfter = `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
 8. Return the ThreadSafetyStatus struct with the collected data.

 Note: We must be cautious about the order of operations and ensure that the worker thread is properly created and joined.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count and safety status before
    ThreadSafetyStatus status;
    status.safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    status.threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create and start worker thread
    std::thread worker([&threadManager]() {
        // Worker thread registers itself
        threadManager.registerThread();
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread count and safety status after
    status.safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    status.threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    return status;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    // Create calculation that depends on reactiveVar and increments recomputeCount
    auto calcResult = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount.value(recomputeCount() + 1);
        return reactiveVar() * 2; // Example calculation: double the value
    });
    
    // Record recompute count before batch
    int countBefore = recomputeCount();
    
    // Execute batch updates
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        reactiveVar.value(reactiveVar() + deltaA);
        reactiveVar.value(reactiveVar() + deltaB);
    }).execute();
    
    // Record recompute count after batch
    int countAfter = recomputeCount();
    
    // Get final value
    int finalVal = calcResult();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        countBefore,
        countAfter,
        finalVal
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {}
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    // Create CacheProbeNode instances
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);
    graph.registerNode(nodeD);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship
    nodeA->addOneObserver(nodeB);
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    nodeB->addOneObserver(nodeC);
    
    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Populate cache data by collecting observers
    graph.collectObservers(nodeA, observers);
    
    // Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = graph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Trigger cache cleanup
    graph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = graph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Close all nodes
    nodeA->close();
    nodeB->close();
    nodeC->close();
    nodeD->close();
    
    // Return cache stats snapshot
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = statsBefore.graphEntries,
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = statsAfter.graphEntries,
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = statsAfter.graphHitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 We need to implement the function `test_thread_manager_manual` as described.

 Steps:
 1. Get thread manager instance: `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance()`
 2. Reset thread manager state: `resetForTesting()`
 3. Register current thread: `registerThread()` (which is called by the macros, but we can call it directly)
 4. Get thread count after registration: `getThreadCount()`
 5. Get thread safety status before enabling: `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
 6. Manually enable thread safety: `enableThreadSafety()`
 7. Get thread safety status after enabling: `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
 8. Reset thread manager state again: `resetForTesting()`
 9. Get thread safety status after reset: `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
10. Return a `ThreadSafetyTransition` struct with the collected data.

 Note: The function `test_thread_manager_manual` is in the namespace `test_reaction`.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyTransition {
    bool beforeEnable;
    bool afterEnable;
    bool afterReset;
    std::size_t threadCount;
};

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    ThreadSafetyTransition transition;
    
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count after registration
    transition.threadCount = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    transition.beforeEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    transition.afterEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    transition.afterReset = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return transition;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>We are given the task to implement the function `test_const_var_propagation` using the `reaction` library.
 The function should:
  1. Create a mutable reactive variable (using `reaction::var`)
  2. Create a constant reactive variable (using `reaction::constVar`)
  3. Create a calculation that sums both variables (using `reaction::calc`)
  4. Get the initial sum (using `get` on the calculation)
  5. Update the mutable reactive variable (using `value` method on the reactive variable)
  6. Get the updated sum
  7. Return a `ConstVarResult` struct with the initial and updated sums

 Steps in code:
  - We'll create a mutable variable (say `mutableVar`) with the initial value `baseValue`.
  - We'll create a constant variable (say `constVar`) with the value `constValue`.
  - We'll create a calculation (say `sumCalc`) that returns the sum of `mutableVar` and `constVar`.
  - We'll get the initial sum by calling `sumCalc.get()`.
  - Then we update the mutable variable by calling `mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta)`.
  - Then we get the updated sum by calling `sumCalc.get()` again.
  - Return the `ConstVarResult` with the two sums.

 Note: The `reaction` library uses a reactive programming model where calculations are updated automatically when their dependencies change.

 However, note that the constant variable is not expected to change, so the calculation should only depend on the mutable variable after the update.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::constVar(constValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that sums both variables
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return mutableVar.get() + constVar.get();
    });
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {

    int recomputeCount = 0;

    calcResult.get();
    
    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = afterInit,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = afterDuplicate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    ThreadGuardState state;
    
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Get thread safety status before guard
    state.safetyBeforeGuard = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 4. Get thread count before guard
    state.threadCountBeforeGuard = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create a worker thread with ThreadRegistrationGuard
    std::thread worker([&]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard guard; // This will register the worker thread
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after guard
    state.safetyAfterGuard = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Get thread count after guard
    state.threadCountAfterGuard = tm.getThreadCount();
    
    return state;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : m_valueField(reaction::var(value)) {
    }
    
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() {
        return m_valueField;
    }
    
private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_valueField;
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    // Create first and second instances
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);
    
    // Read IDs
    uint64_t firstIdFirst = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecond = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();
    
    // Update reactive fields to avoid unused variable warnings
    first.valueField().value(100);
    second.valueField().value(200);
    
    // Return comparison
    return FieldIdComparison{firstIdFirst, firstIdSecond, secondId};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

};

ResourceSBOState test_resource_sbo() {
    // Define small and large resource types
    using SmallType = int;
    using LargeType = std::array<int, 100>;
    
    // Create resource instances
    reaction::Resource<SmallType> small_resource{0};
    reaction::Resource<LargeType> large_resource{};
    
    // Query SBO usage
    bool small_sbo = small_resource.isUsingSBO();
    bool large_sbo = large_resource.isUsingSBO();
    
    return ResourceSBOState{small_sbo, large_sbo};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>We are given the task to implement the function `test_last_handle_restore` using the `reaction` library.
 The function must perform the following steps:
 1. Create a reactive variable (using `reaction::var`)
 2. Create a calculation with LastHandle policy (using `reaction::calc` and specifying the policy as `reaction::LastHandle`)
 3. Get the initial result of the calculation
 4. Update the source variable
 5. Get the updated result of the calculation
 6. Close the source node (using `close` method on the reactive variable)
 7. Get the result after the source is closed
 8. Return a `LastHandleSnapshot` struct containing the three results

 We note that the `LastHandle` policy is used in the calculation, which means that when the source node is closed, the calculation should still return the last known value.

 Steps in code:

 - We'll create a reactive variable (say, `sourceVar`) with the initial value.
 - Then we create a calculation that depends on `sourceVar` and uses the `LastHandle` policy. The calculation can be a simple identity function, or we can do something like doubling the value to show the update.
 - We get the initial value of the calculation.
 - Then we update the source variable to a new value.
 - We get the updated value of the calculation.
 - Then we close the source variable (which would normally break the dependency, but because of LastHandle, the calculation should keep the last value).
 - We get the value of the calculation after closing the source.

 However, note that the `LastHandle` policy is applied to the calculation. When the source is closed, the calculation will no longer react to changes, but it will retain the last computed value.

 Let's write the code accordingly.

 Important: We must include the necessary headers and use the `reaction` namespace.

 Since the function is in the `test_reaction` namespace, we will define it there.

 We assume that the `reaction` library is already included by the given header.

 Let's implement the function.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable
    auto sourceVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create a calculation with LastHandle policy
    auto calcWithLastHandle = reaction::calc<reaction::LastHandle>([&]() {
        return sourceVar.get() * 2; // Example calculation: double the value
    });
    
    // Get the initial result
    int initialResult = calcWithLastHandle.get();
    
    // Update the source variable
    sourceVar.value(updatedValue);
    
    // Get the updated result
    int updatedResult = calcWithLastHandle.get();
    
    // Close the source node
    sourceVar.close();
    
    // Get the result after source close
    int resultAfterClose = calcWithLastHandle.get();
    
    // Return the snapshot
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = resultAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UniqueIdSequence {
    uint64_t firstValue;
    uint64_t secondValue;
    uint64_t thirdValue;
    bool strictlyIncreasing;
};

UniqueIdSequence test_unique_id_sequence() {
    // Create three UniqueID instances
    reaction::UniqueID id1;
    reaction::UniqueID id2;
    reaction::UniqueID id3;
    
    // Get values from each instance
    uint64_t first = id1.getValue();
    uint64_t second = id2.getValue();
    uint64_t third = id3.getValue();
    
    // Check if the sequence is strictly increasing
    bool increasing = (first < second) && (second < third);
    
    // Return the sequence with the values and the check result
    return UniqueIdSequence{first, second, third, increasing};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Create a Resource instance with initial value
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    
    // Variable to track if the operation reported a change
    bool changeReported = false;
    
    // Perform atomic operation: add delta to the value
    resource.atomicOperation([&](int& value) -> bool {
        value += delta;
        // Assume the operation reports change if the value actually changed
        changeReported = true;
        return true; // Explicitly report change
    });
    
    // Get the final value
    int finalValue = resource.get();
    
    // Return the result
    return ResourceAtomicResult{finalValue, changeReported};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report{};
    
    // 1. Disable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // 3. Create report struct - already done
    
    // 4. Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // 5. Create StackGuard in a scope block
    bool guardAllocated = false;
    std::size_t usageDuring = 0;
    double percentage = 0.0;
    bool nearLimit = false;
    
    {
        // Create StackGuard with a fixed size (e.g., 1024 bytes)
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<1024> guard;
        
        // 6. Check if guard was successfully allocated
        guardAllocated = guard.allocated;
        
        if (guardAllocated) {
            // 7. Get current usage during guard
            usageDuring = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
            
            // 8. Get statistics
            auto stats = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
            percentage = stats.usagePercentage;
            nearLimit = stats.nearLimit;
        }
    } // 10. Exit guard scope
    
    report.guardAllocated = guardAllocated;
    report.usageDuringGuard = usageDuring;
    report.usagePercentage = percentage;
    report.nearLimitDuringGuard = nearLimit;
    
    // 11. Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
    
    // 12. Disable monitoring again
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // 13. Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConditionalMutexResult {
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety;
    bool tryLockWithSafety;
};

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager &threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Create ConditionalMutex instance
    reaction::ConditionalMutex conditionalMutex;
    
    // 4. Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block (without safety enabled)
    bool lockWithoutSafety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalMutex> guard(conditionalMutex);
        // 5. Attempt to lock mutex without safety enabled
        // The lock should be a no-op since thread safety is disabled by default after reset

    return {lockWithoutSafety, lockWithSafety};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    // Step 1: Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 2: Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // Step 3: Create ConditionalSharedMutex instance
    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex sharedMutex;
    
    // Step 4: Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety (thread safety disabled)
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety = sharedMutex.try_lock_shared();
    
    // Step 5: Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        sharedMutex.unlock_shared();
    }
    
    // Step 6: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> guard(sharedMutex);
        
        // Step 7: Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held without safety
        bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = sharedMutex.try_lock();
        
        // Step 8: Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            sharedMutex.unlock();
        }
    } // Step 9: Exit guard scope (shared lock is automatically released)
    
    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 11: Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety (thread safety enabled)
    bool trySharedWithSafety = sharedMutex.try_lock_shared();
    
    // Step 12: Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (trySharedWithSafety) {
        sharedMutex.unlock_shared();
    }
    
    // Step 13: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block again

        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> guard(sharedMutex);

        // Step 15: Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            sharedMutex.unlock();
        }
    } // Step 16: Exit guard scope
    
    // Step 17: Return shared mutex result
    return SharedMutexResult{
        trySharedWithoutSafety,
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety,
        trySharedWithSafety,
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct TinyPod {
    int a;
    int b;
};

struct MediumPod {
    double values[3];
};

struct LargePod {
    std::byte payload[128];
};

struct SboTraitSummary {
    bool tinyAlwaysSbo;
    bool tinyForceHeap;
    bool mediumConditionalSbo;
    bool mediumForceHeap;
    bool largeForceHeap;
    std::size_t alwaysThreshold;
    std::size_t conditionalThreshold;
    std::size_t maxStackUsage;
};

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    // Get SBO traits for each type
    auto tiny_traits = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>;
    auto medium_traits = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>;
    auto large_traits = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<LargePod>;
    
    // Get configuration thresholds
    auto config = reaction::memory::get_sbo_config();
    
    // Populate summary
    SboTraitSummary summary;
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = tiny_traits.always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = tiny_traits.force_heap;
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = medium_traits.conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = medium_traits.force_heap;
    summary.largeForceHeap = large_traits.force_heap;
    summary.alwaysThreshold = config.ALWAYS_SBO_SIZE;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = config.CONDITIONAL_SBO_SIZE;
    summary.maxStackUsage = config.MAX_SBO_STACK_USAGE;
    
    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {

    int beforeResetValue = calculation();
    
    // Step 6: Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 7: Check if thread safety is enabled
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Step 8: Reset calculation logic
    calculation.reset([&reactiveVar, multiplier]() {
        return reactiveVar() * (multiplier + 1); // Change multiplier to multiplier+1
    });
    
    // Step 9: Update source variable
    reactiveVar.value(initialValue + 10); // Update with new value
    
    // Step 10: Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calculation();
    
    // Step 11: Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        beforeResetValue,
        afterResetValue,
        safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>We are given the function signature and the requirements. We need to implement the function `describe_error_codes` in the namespace `test_reaction`.
 The function should:
  1. Create a vector to store descriptions.
  2. Reserve space for optimization (the size is the number of input codes).
  3. Iterate through each error code in the input vector.
  4. Convert each code to a string using the `reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString` function.
  5. Return the vector of descriptions.

 Steps:
  - We are including the necessary headers: <reaction/core/exception.h> and <string> and <vector>.
  - We are in the namespace `test_reaction`.

 Implementation:

 We'll create a vector of strings, reserve the space equal to the size of the input vector to avoid multiple allocations.
 Then we loop over each error code, convert it to a string using the static member function `reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString`.
 We push back each converted string into the vector.

 Note: The function `errorCodeToString` is static, so we can call it by the class name.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    
    for (const auto& code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(code));
    }
    
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    // Create ReactionException instance
    reaction::ReactionException exception(message, code, file, line, function);

    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = exception.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = exception.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = exception.getFile();
    info.line = exception.getLine();
    info.function = exception.getFunction();

    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    using namespace reaction;
    
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto source = var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // 8. Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables and set names
    auto var1 = reaction::var(0);
    var1.setName(firstName);
    
    auto var2 = reaction::var(0);
    var2.setName(secondName);
    
    auto var3 = reaction::var(0);
    var3.setName(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into set to get ordered collection
    std::set<reaction::React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    orderedNames.reserve(reactSet.size());
    
    for (const auto &react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique (should be since set enforces uniqueness)
    bool allUnique = reactSet.size() == 3;
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {
        // Initialize the node with a name
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }
    
    void clear() {
        m_notificationCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName,
                                      const std::string &targetName) {
    // Step 1: Create source and target nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Register nodes
    graph.addNode(source);
    graph.addNode(target);
    
    // Step 4: Set up observer relationship (source observes target)
    graph.addObserver(sourceName, targetName);
    
    // Step 5: Trigger notification on source
    source->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 6: Check if target was notified before reset
    bool notifiedBeforeReset = (target->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 7: Clear target notification count
    target->clear();
    
    // Step 8: Reset target node
    graph.resetNode(targetName);
    
    // Step 9: Trigger notification on source again
    source->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 10: Check if target was notified after reset
    bool notifiedAfterReset = (target->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 11: Close nodes
    graph.closeNode(sourceName);
    graph.closeNode(targetName);
    
    // Step 12: Return reset node result
    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBeforeReset, notifiedAfterReset};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate StackMonitor::canAllocate behaviour across modes.
 *
 * @param largeSize Value used for runtime canAllocate after release
 * @return StackAllocationSnapshot Snapshot of allocation checks before/after guard
 */
inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot snapshot;
    
    // 1. Disable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // 3. Check allocation without monitoring
    snapshot.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(16);
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(64);
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    // 4. Enable monitoring
    reaction::StackMonitor::enableMonitoring();
    
    // 5. Initialize stack monitor again
    reaction::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // 6. Check large allocation with monitoring
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(64);
    
    // 7. Create StackGuard in scope block
    bool guardSuccess = false;
    bool blockedAtLimit = false;
    {
        reaction::StackGuard<64> guard; // Using fixed size for testing
        guardSuccess = guard.success();
        
        // 9. Check if allocation blocked at limit
        blockedAtLimit = !reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(largeSize);
    }
    snapshot.guardSucceeded = guardSuccess;
    snapshot.allocationBlockedAtLimit = blockedAtLimit;
    
    // 11. Check runtime allocation after release
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = reaction::StackMonitor::canAllocate(largeSize);
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }
    
    void clear() {
        m_notifications = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Register nodes
    observerGraph.addNode(owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(field);
    
    // Step 4: Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 5: Register field nodes under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObjField(objectId, field);
    
    // Step 6: Bind field to owner
    observerGraph.addObserver(field, owner);
    
    // Step 7: Trigger notification on field
    field->notify();
    
    // Step 8: Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 9: Clear owner notification count
    owner->clear();
    
    // Step 10: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.closeNode(owner);
    
    // Step 11: Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.deleteObjField(objectId);
    
    // Step 12: Attempt to bind field again
    observerGraph.addObserver(field, owner);
    
    // Step 13: Trigger notification on field again
    field->notify();
    
    // Step 14: Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 15: Close nodes
    owner->close();
    field->close();
    
    // Step 16: Return field graph cleanup result
    return {notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    // Step 1: Enable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::enableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Get usage statistics before guard
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBefore = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
    
    // Step 4: Check if template allocation is allowed
    bool templateAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate<reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<64 * 1024>>();
    
    // Step 5: Check if runtime allocation is allowed
    bool runtimeAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate(32 * 1024);
    
    // Step 6: Create large StackGuard instance
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<64 * 1024> guard;
    
    // Step 7: Check if guard succeeded
    bool guardSucceeded = guard.success();
    
    // Step 8: Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    bool allocationBlocked = !reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate(16 * 1024);
    
    // Step 9: Get usage statistics after guard
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfter = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
    
    // Step 10: Return stack limit snapshot
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBefore,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfter,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardSucceeded,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlocked
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // 1. Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto resource = reaction::Resource<Void>::create();
    
    // 2. Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool uses_sbo = resource->isUsingSBO();
    
    // 3. Get value from resource
    auto value = resource->getValue();
    
    // 4. Check if retrieved value is Void type
    bool is_void = std::is_same_v<decltype(value), reaction::Void>;
    
    // 5. Get observer graph instance
    auto &observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Register resource node
    observer_graph.addNode(resource->shared_from_this());
    
    // 7. Assign node name
    observer_graph.setName(resource->shared_from_this(), nodeName);
    
    // 8. Check if name was assigned
    bool name_assigned = (observer_graph.getName(resource->shared_from_this()) == nodeName);
    
    // 9. Close node
    observer_graph.closeNode(resource->shared_from_this());
    
    // 10. Check if name was cleared after close
    bool name_cleared = (observer_graph.getName(resource->shared_from_this()).empty());
    
    // 11. Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = is_void,
        .usesSboStorage = uses_sbo,
        .nameAssigned = name_assigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = name_cleared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {

    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    // Step 1: Create CountingObserver instance
    CountingObserver countingObserver;
    
    // Step 2: Create three source ObserverNode instances
    auto source1 = std::make_unique<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_unique<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_unique<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 4: Register all nodes
    graph.registerNode(countingObserver.shared_from_this());
    graph.registerNode(source1);
    graph.registerNode(source2);
    graph.registerNode(source3);
    
    // Step 5: Attach single observer (CountingObserver) to first source
    countingObserver.attach(source1);
    
    // Step 6: Trigger notification on first source
    source1->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 7: Get notification count after initial setup
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // Step 8: Rewire dependencies - detach from source1 and attach to source2
    countingObserver.attach(source2);
    
    // Step 9: Trigger notification on old source (source1)
    source1->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 10: Get notification count after old source notification
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // Step 11: Trigger notification on second source
    source2->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 12: Get notification count after second source notification
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // Step 13: Trigger notification on third source
    source3->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 14: Get notification count after third source notification
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = countingObserver.notifications();
    
    // Step 15: Close all nodes
    countingObserver.close();
    source1.close();
    source2.close();
    source3.close();
    
    // Step 16: Return observer node link snapshot
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstNotificationsAfterInitial,
        firstNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        secondNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    // Create reactive variable initialized to 0
    auto reactive_var = reaction::var(0);
    
    // Create counter for calculation evaluations
    int calc_evaluations = 0;
    
    // Create calculation that tracks value and increments evaluation counter
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        calc_evaluations++;
        return reactive_var.get();
    });
    
    // Apply atomic addition and get value
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(reactive_var, 5);
    int after_addition = calc.get();
    
    // Apply atomic multiplication and get value
    reaction::atomicMulAssign(reactive_var, 3);
    int after_multiplication = calc.get();
    
    // Apply atomic bitwise AND and get value
    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(reactive_var, 0x0F);
    int after_bitwise_and = calc.get();
    
    // Apply atomic left shift and get value
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(reactive_var, 2);
    int after_shift = calc.get();
    
    // Apply post-increment and get return value
    int post_increment_return = reactive_var.postIncrement();
    
    // Get final value
    int final_value = reactive_var.get();
    
    // Return snapshot with collected values
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        after_addition,
        after_multiplication,
        after_bitwise_and,
        after_shift,
        post_increment_return,
        final_value,
        calc_evaluations
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
